info.title: Trinity Cognitive Probes
info.description:
# Trinity Cognitive Probes

Benchmark dataset for evaluating AI cognitive capabilities across 5 brain-inspired tracks:

- **THLP** (Track 1): Hippocampal Learning Probe — Few-shot learning, belief update, error-driven learning, reward signals, long-context retention
- **TMP** (Track 2): Metacognition Probe — Confidence calibration, error detection, strategic adaptation, knowledge boundaries, monitoring under load  
- **TAGP** (Track 3): Attentional Gateway Probe — Selective filtering, sustained attention, attention shifting, adversarial needle, divided attention
- **TEFB** (Track 4): Executive Function Battery — Multi-step planning, Stroop-like inhibition, Wisconsin card sorting, working memory span, conflicting instructions
- **TSCP** (Track 5): Social Cognition Probe — Theory of mind (false belief), pragmatic inference, audience adaptation, negotiation, implicit social norms

## Files
- `thlp_learning.csv` — 100 items for learning benchmarks
- `tmp_test.csv` — 100 items for metacognition benchmarks
- `tagp_attention.csv` — 100 items for attention benchmarks
- `tefb_executive.csv` — 100 items for executive function benchmarks
- `tscp_social.csv` — 100 items for social cognition benchmarks

## Format
Each CSV contains cognitive test items with:
- `id`: Unique item identifier
- `task`: Sub-task name
- `question`: Test prompt
- `answer`: Expected response
- Additional columns per track (difficulty, brain_zone, neural_analog, etc.)

## Trinity Framework
Part of the Trinity AGI benchmarking framework — φ-scaled difficulty using Fibonacci sequences [3, 5, 8, 13, 21] for biologically-plausible cognitive gradients.
